title: RESY tutorial

title: RESY tutorial
text: example data include species data vegetation surveys sites data information location surveys. First, vegetation surveys: Second, sites data:
code: data_species <- read_csv(system.file("extdata", "data_example_species.csv", package = "RESY")) data_species #> # A tibble: 3,580 × 4 #>     ...1 RELEVE_NR cover species                                              #>    <dbl> <chr>     <dbl> <chr>                                                #>  1     1 HU32        0.1 Dryopteris filix-mas (L.) Schott                     #>  2     2 HU32        0.1 Cephalanthera longifolia (L.) Fritsch                #>  3     3 HU32        0.5 Prenanthes purpurea L.                               #>  4     4 HU32        0.5 Anemone nemorosa L.                                  #>  5     5 HU32        0.5 Epipactis helleborine (L.) Crantz subsp. helleborine #>  6     6 HU32        0.5 Sorbus aucuparia L. subsp. aucuparia                 #>  7     7 HU32       87.5 Fagus sylvatica L.                                   #>  8     8 HU32        3   Rubus hirtus Waldst. & Kit.                          #>  9     9 HU32        3   Abies alba Mill.                                     #> 10    10 HU32        3   Oxalis acetosella L.                                 #> # ℹ 3,570 more rows data_sites <- read_csv(    system.file("extdata", "data_example_sites.csv", package = "RESY")    ) |>    select(-"cover") |>    st_as_sf(coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) data_sites #> Simple feature collection with 200 features and 2 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 9.809499 ymin: 42.32483 xmax: 12.08963 ymax: 44.32464 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 200 × 3 #>     ...1 RELEVE_NR            geometry #>  * <dbl> <chr>             <POINT [°]> #>  1     1 JZ37       (9.93798 44.32464) #>  2     2 FR49      (11.09287 43.91546) #>  3     3 PT45      (11.65339 43.09104) #>  4     4 ZH63      (10.53301 43.73897) #>  5     5 TF93      (10.40239 44.23978) #>  6     6 KG68      (10.40203 44.24087) #>  7     7 QJ27       (10.40635 44.2413) #>  8     8 JN90      (10.40602 44.24296) #>  9     9 ZM18      (10.66071 44.12734) #> 10    10 BQ20       (10.6589 44.12456) #> # ℹ 190 more rows

title: RESY tutorial
text: vegetation surveys example dataset Tuscany Italy.  see part Italy, mainly Tuscany Mediterranean specific Tyrrhenian Sea. dots locations vegetation surveys. islands.

title: RESY tutorial
text: First, prepare sites species data prepare_eunis() three warnings: column Altitude (m) missing covered prepare_eunis(), provide vignette("Get altitude data"). NAs ecoregions (Ecoreg) country (Country) columns. vegetation surveys islands covered base map.
code: outcome <- RESY::prepare_eunis(    data = data_sites,    source_crs = 25832,    run_taxonomy = TRUE,    species_data = data_species,    run_coast_dunes = TRUE,    coast_buffer = 5000    ) #> Warning in RESY::prepare_eunis(data = data_sites, source_crs = 25832, #> run_taxonomy = TRUE, : The column 'Altitude (m)' is missing. #> Warning in RESY::prepare_eunis(data = data_sites, source_crs = 25832, #> run_taxonomy = TRUE, : NAs in 'Ecoreg' after spatial lookup. #> Warning in RESY::prepare_eunis(data = data_sites, source_crs = 25832, #> run_taxonomy = TRUE, : NAs in 'Country' after spatial lookup. #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries outcome_sites <- tibble(outcome$sites) outcome_species <- tibble(outcome$species)

text: Chytrý M, Řezníčková M, Novotný P et al. (2024) FloraVeg.EU – online database European vegetation, habitats flora. – Applied Vegetation Science 27, e12798. https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12798 Bruelheide H, Tichý L, Chytrý M, Jansen F (2021) Implementing formal language vegetation classification expert systems (ESy) statistical computing environment R. – Applied Vegetation Science 24, e12562 https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12562 Chytrý M, Tichý L, Hennekens SM et al. (2020) EUNIS Habitat Classification: expert system, characteristic species combinations distribution maps European habitats. – Applied Vegetation Science 23, 648–675. https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12519 Mucina L, Bültmann H, Dierßen K et al. (2016) Vegetation Europe: hierarchical floristic classification system vascular plant, bryophyte, lichen, algal communities. – Applied Vegetation Science 19(Suppl. 1), 3–264.https://doi.org/10.1111/avsc.12257

what: Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
title: Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
text: Flags points based whether fall within buffer coastline within known coastal dune formations. Input must sf object EPSG:25832. Returns flag columns integration main EUNIS workflow.

title: Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
text: data_sf sf object containing point geometries EPSG:25832. buffer_dist Numeric. distance (meters) buffer coastline. Default 5000. Must single positive value.

title: Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
text: tibble two columns: Coast_EEA "coastal" point within buffered coastline, "non_coastal" otherwise. Dunes_Bohn "Y_DUNES" point intersects dune area, "N_DUNES" otherwise.

title: Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
text: function performs following steps: Crops coastline dune spatial layers bounding box points. Buffers coastline user-defined buffer_dist. Performs spatial intersections identify points within coastal dune zones.

title: Check if Points Intersect Coastline or Dune Areas (EPSG:25832) — check_coast_dunes
text: function requires following files: coastline_epsg25832.rda (object co) dunes_bohn_500mbuffer_epsg25832.rda (object bohn)

what: Transform the coordinates into UTM32N — check_coordinates
title: Transform the coordinates into UTM32N — check_coordinates
text: check_coordinates() used transform coordinates coordination system UTM zone 32. Additionally, data ia data frame, transformed sf object.

title: Transform the coordinates into UTM32N — check_coordinates
Transform the coordinates into UTM32N — check_coordinates
text: data Input data frame tibble columns 'Longitude' 'Latitude' sf object source_crs Coordination system EPSG ID original data

title: Transform the coordinates into UTM32N — check_coordinates
text: point sf object coorination system UTM zone 32N / EPSG:25832 length data

title: Transform the coordinates into UTM32N — check_coordinates
code: data <- tibble::tibble(    plot = "1a", Latitude = "48.40035", Longitude = 11.72014    )    check_coordinates(data, source_crs = 4326) #> Coordination system transformed to ETRS89 / UTM zone 32N / EPSG:25832 #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 1 field #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 701326.4 ymin: 5364374 xmax: 701326.4 ymax: 5364374 #> Projected CRS: ETRS89 / UTM zone 32N #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   plot            geometry #> * <chr>        <POINT [m]> #> 1 1a    (701326.4 5364374)

code: data <- tibble::tibble(       plot = "1a", Latitude = "701327", Longitude = 5364375       ) |>       sf::st_as_sf() #> Error in st_sf(x, ..., agr = agr, sf_column_name = sf_column_name): no simple features geometry column present    check_ecoregion(data) #> Error in check_ecoregion(data): could not find function "check_ecoregion"

what: Prepare vegetation plot data for EUNIS expert system classification — prepare_eunis
title: Prepare vegetation plot data for EUNIS expert system classification — prepare_eunis
text: Prepares vegetation plot header data input EUNIS expert classification system. Automatically validates coordinates, converts CRS EPSG:25832, assigns WWF ecoregions country identifiers, optionally adds dune/coast indicators taxonomic harmonization.

title: Prepare vegetation plot data for EUNIS expert system classification — prepare_eunis
code: prepare_eunis(   data,   source_crs = NULL,   run_taxonomy = FALSE,   species_data = NULL,   run_coast_dunes = FALSE,   coast_buffer = 5000 )

title: Prepare vegetation plot data for EUNIS expert system classification — prepare_eunis
text: data data frame sf object containing vegetation plot data. sf object, columns named Longitude Latitude must exist. source_crs Integer EPSG code original coordinate reference system. Required data plain data frame; ignored data sf. run_taxonomy Logical; TRUE, applies check_taxonomy() species data. species_data Optional data frame column species. Required run_taxonomy = TRUE. run_coast_dunes Logical; TRUE, computes coastal dune flags. coast_buffer Numeric buffer (meters) coastline proximity. Default 5000.

title: Prepare vegetation plot data for EUNIS expert system classification — prepare_eunis
text: list : sites Data frame harmonised plot data, geometry dropped. species_checked Output check_taxonomy() run; otherwise NULL.

what: Check for the completeness of the data frame for running the EUNIS expert system — check_format
title: Check for the completeness of the data frame for running the EUNIS expert system — check_format
text: check_format() used run specific functions package get bring data required shape (coordination system: UTM zone 32N) get required data: coastal position, dune location, ecoregion, country. Furthermore, checked coordinates altitude values data frame.

title: Check for the completeness of the data frame for running the EUNIS expert system — check_format
Check for the completeness of the data frame for running the EUNIS expert system — check_format
text: data Input data frame, tibble point sf object source_crs coordination system original data EPSG ID

title: Check for the completeness of the data frame for running the EUNIS expert system — check_format
text: sf object length data required columns EUNIS expert system: 'RELEVE_NR', 'Altitude (m)', 'Coast_EEA', 'Dunes_Bohn', 'Ecoreg', 'Country'.

title: Check for the completeness of the data frame for running the EUNIS expert system — check_format
code: data <- tibble::tibble(       plot = "1a", Latitude = "701327", Longitude = 5364375       ) |>       sf::st_as_sf() #> Error in st_sf(x, ..., agr = agr, sf_column_name = sf_column_name): no simple features geometry column present    check_format(data, source_crs = 25832) #> Error in check_format(data, source_crs = 25832): could not find function "check_format"

what: Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
title: Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
text: function takes data frame species names validates Taxonomic Name Resolution Service (TNRS) using World Checklist Vascular Plants (WCVP) primary source.

title: Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
text: species data frame containing column named species botanical names checked.

title: Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
text: data frame containing first two columns original input taxonomic match result (5th column TNRS output).

title: Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
text: function submits species list TNRS API. specifically retrieves submitted name matched taxonomic results, joins back original data frame. Note function relies positional indexing TNRS results (columns 2, 5, 34), assumes TNRS API output format remains consistent.

title: Resolve Species Taxonomy via TNRS — check_taxonomy
text: function uses hard-coded column indices (c(2, 5, 34)) subset TNRS results. TNRS API TNRS package updates output structure, function may require manual adjustment.

what: Add a new classification from a TXT expert file — resy_add_classification
title: Add a new classification from a TXT expert file — resy_add_classification
text: Copies TXT RESY classification store generates corresponding JSON RDS. default, writes user storage (recommended). Writing installed package directory typically requires writable library often possible.

title: Add a new classification from a TXT expert file — resy_add_classification
code: resy_add_classification(   txtfile,   scheme,   version,   location = c("user", "package"),   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Add a new classification from a TXT expert file — resy_add_classification
text: txtfile Path expert TXT file. scheme Scheme name store . version Version identifier store . location store ("user" recommended). overwrite Logical; overwrite existing files.

title: Add a new classification from a TXT expert file — resy_add_classification
text: named list created file paths.

what: Aggregate taxa to expert-system aggregation level — resy_aggregate_taxa
title: Aggregate taxa to expert-system aggregation level — resy_aggregate_taxa
text: Replaces obs$TaxonName aggregated names occur aggregation mapping expert system. use GermanSl EuroSL, use taxval instead.

Aggregate taxa to expert-system aggregation level — resy_aggregate_taxa

List available classifications — resy_available_classifications

title: List available classifications — resy_available_classifications

what: List possible assignments per plot — resy_candidates
title: List possible assignments per plot — resy_candidates
text: result object returned resy_classify() contains long table vegetation types evaluated possible plot (.e. corresponding vegtype formula evaluated TRUE). helper lets access, sort, filter candidates.

title: List possible assignments per plot — resy_candidates
code: resy_candidates(   res,   plot_id = NULL,   priority = NULL,   min_priority = NULL,   top_n = NULL )

title: List possible assignments per plot — resy_candidates
text: res resy_result returned resy_classify(). plot_id Optional plot id (character numeric). NULL, returns candidates plots. priority Optional priority rank (integer). provided, candidates priority_rank == priority returned. min_priority Optional minimum priority rank (integer). Higher means higher priority. provided, candidates priority_rank >= min_priority returned. top_n Optional maximum number candidates return per plot.

title: List possible assignments per plot — resy_candidates
text: data.table columns plot_id, type, priority, priority_rank.

title: Classification storage roots — resy_classifications_root
text: Returns root directory classifications stored. Built-classifications ship inside package (read-install). User-added classifications stored user-writable data directory.

title: Classification storage roots — resy_classifications_root
Classification storage roots — resy_classifications_root
text: location One "user" "package". create Logical; create directory (applies location="user").

title: Classification storage roots — resy_classifications_root
text: path.

what: Classify vegetation plots with the expert system — resy_classify
title: Classify vegetation plots with the expert system — resy_classify
text: Convenience wrapper loads expert system (file scheme/version), aggregates taxa, evaluates membership conditions/expressions, returns plot classifications.

title: Classify vegetation plots with the expert system — resy_classify
code: resy_classify(   obs,   header,   expertfile = NULL,   scheme = "EUNIS",   version = NULL,   location = c("user", "package"),   id_col = NULL,   mc = max(1L, floor(parallel::detectCores() * 0.75)) )

title: Classify vegetation plots with the expert system — resy_classify
text: obs Observations data.table least columns describing plot id, TaxonName, Cover_Perc. header Plot header data.frame; required evaluating $$C/$$N. expertfile Optional path expert-system file (.rds/.json/.txt). set, takes precedence. scheme Scheme name (default: "EUNIS"). version Version identifier. NULL, uses latest available version scheme. location look scheme/version "user" "package". id_col Optional name plot id column. NULL, tries PlotObservationID, RELEVE_NR, PlotID. mc Number CPU cores use.

title: Classify vegetation plots with the expert system — resy_classify
text: object class resy_result.

what: Compile a classification to RDS — resy_compile_expert
title: Compile a classification to RDS — resy_compile_expert
text: Creates (updates) expert.rds given classification version. compiled RDS stored selected location (default: user storage).

title: Compile a classification to RDS — resy_compile_expert
code: resy_compile_expert(   scheme,   version,   location = c("user", "package"),   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Compile a classification to RDS — resy_compile_expert
text: scheme Scheme name. version Version identifier. location write compiled RDS ("user" recommended). overwrite Logical; overwrite existing expert.rds.

title: Compile a classification to RDS — resy_compile_expert
text: compiled object (invisibly).
